description:
You are playing the role of an infrastructure engineer who has just received a small CSV-style host catalogue that was exported from the provisioning system.  
Your goal is to translate that catalogue into two neatly formatted text files using nothing but standard **awk** and **sed** command-line utilities (you may combine them with pipes, loops, redirection, etc., but do not resort to perl, python, jq, etc.).

1. Pre-existing material  
   • A directory already exists:  
     /home/user/provision  
   • Inside that directory there is a CSV file called **servers.list** whose exact contents are:  
     ```
     web-1,10.1.1.10,ubuntu18
     web-2,10.1.1.11,ubuntu18
     db-1,10.1.2.20,centos7
     cache-1,10.1.3.30,alpine3
     msg-queue,10.1.4.40,debian10
     ```  

2. What you must create  
   Make a new sub-directory:  
   /home/user/provision/output  

   Inside that sub-directory create the following two files (the names and the exact byte-for-byte contents shown below are what the automated test will check):

   a) /home/user/provision/output/host_inventory.cfg  
      Required file format (5 lines, no trailing spaces, in the same order as the original CSV):  
      ```
      web-1 ansible_host=10.1.1.10 ansible_user=root
      web-2 ansible_host=10.1.1.11 ansible_user=root
      db-1 ansible_host=10.1.2.20 ansible_user=root
      cache-1 ansible_host=10.1.3.30 ansible_user=root
      msg-queue ansible_host=10.1.4.40 ansible_user=root
      ```

   b) /home/user/provision/output/distro_summary.log  
      Required file format (4 lines, alphabetically sorted by distribution name, again no trailing spaces):  
      ```
      Distribution: alpine3 Hosts: 1
      Distribution: centos7 Hosts: 1
      Distribution: debian10 Hosts: 1
      Distribution: ubuntu18 Hosts: 2
      ```

3. Constraints & verification hints  
   • Use **awk** and/or **sed** for all text processing.  
   • Do not hard-code the answers; derive them from /home/user/provision/servers.list.  
   • The automated grader will verify:  
     – the existence of /home/user/provision/output  
     – the exact names and byte-for-byte contents of the two files listed above  
     – that only standard Unix text utilities (awk, sed, sort, cut, etc.) were used.  

4. Deliverable  
   When you are finished, the two files under /home/user/provision/output must exactly match the formats given above so that an automated diff can succeed.  
   Feel free to display intermediary results to the terminal, but remember that the final output must live in the two files whose paths and formats are specified here.
truth:
Initial on-disk state before the agent begins:

1. A directory /home/user/provision already exists and is writable by the current user.
2. File /home/user/provision/servers.list exists with the following 5 lines, LF-terminated:
   web-1,10.1.1.10,ubuntu18
   web-2,10.1.1.11,ubuntu18
   db-1,10.1.2.20,centos7
   cache-1,10.1.3.30,alpine3
   msg-queue,10.1.4.40,debian10

Expected final state after the agent completes:

1. Directory /home/user/provision/output exists (mode 755 or 700 accepted).
2. File /home/user/provision/output/host_inventory.cfg exists and contains exactly (including newlines, with no extra blank line at end):
   web-1 ansible_host=10.1.1.10 ansible_user=root
   web-2 ansible_host=10.1.1.11 ansible_user=root
   db-1 ansible_host=10.1.2.20 ansible_user=root
   cache-1 ansible_host=10.1.3.30 ansible_user=root
   msg-queue ansible_host=10.1.4.40 ansible_user=root

3. File /home/user/provision/output/distro_summary.log exists and contains exactly:
   Distribution: alpine3 Hosts: 1
   Distribution: centos7 Hosts: 1
   Distribution: debian10 Hosts: 1
   Distribution: ubuntu18 Hosts: 2
